summary: Partially decompiled the fighter common file `ftCo_09F7.c`, replacing `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` stubs for `ftCo_8009F834` and `ftCo_800A0098` with C that reached a reported 65.13% file match. The main work reconstructs a large effect-spawning dispatcher keyed by anim/effect IDs, using fighter part joints, random position offsets, floor-normal angles, facing direction, and calls into `efAsync_Spawn`. The PR also tightened nearby prototypes in `ftCo_09F7.h`, `ftCo_0A01`, and `ftCo_0B3E`, exposing `ftCo_800B46B8` and replacing placeholder/unknown signatures with concrete `int`, `Fighter_Part`, and `void` types where needed.
what_changed: `ftCo_8009F834` was changed from a `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` stub into a large partially matched implementation that dispatches many `arg1` effect/animlist IDs through explicit branch blocks and calls `efAsync_Spawn` with varying effect kinds and argument layouts.
`ftCo_800A0098` was implemented as a short command sequence calling `ftCo_800B46B8` with IDs `0x80`, `0x81`, `0x8E`, `0x91`, then ending with `ftCo_800B463C(arg0, 0x7F)`.
`ftCo_8009F834` signature was refined from generic `ftCo_GObj*, int, int, int, int, Vec3*, Vec3*, float` to use `Fighter_GObj*` in the C file and `Fighter_Part` for the part argument in the header.
`ftCo_09F7.c` gained real dependencies needed by the implementation: `ftCo_0B3E.h`, `ef/efasync.h`, `ft/fighter.h`, `ft/ftparts.h`, `ft/inlines.h`, `ft/types.h`, `<trigf.h>`, and `<baselib/gobj.h>`.
`ftCo_0B3E.h` changed `ftCo_800B463C` from `void ftCo_800B463C(UNK_T, int)` to `void ftCo_800B463C(int, int)` and added an external prototype for `void ftCo_800B46B8(int, int, int)`.
`ftCo_0B3E.c` removed local placeholder implementations for `ftCo_800B463C` and `ftCo_800B46B8`, replacing them with decomp placeholder markers `/// #ftCo_800B463C` and `/// #ftCo_800B46B8`.
`ftCo_800A0C8C` in `ftCo_0A01.c/.h` was tightened from an unknown argument/return style to `void ftCo_800A0C8C(int arg0)`, matching its simple call to `ftCo_800B463C(arg0, 127)`.
smart_moves: The implementation keeps the m2c-style control flow with `goto block_N` labels rather than prematurely restructuring a large dispatcher, which is a practical tactic for improving match percentage while preserving compiler-shaped branch structure.

The PR introduced only the concrete headers needed for the newly referenced systems, allowing the C to use real fighter, part, effect, random, and trig APIs instead of relying entirely on placeholder declarations.

Prototype cleanup was done across caller and callee headers at the same time as the new implementation, reducing mismatch pressure from `UNK_T`, `UNK_PARAMS`, and hidden static placeholder declarations.

The effect spawn cases reuse existing fighter access patterns such as `gobj->user_data`, `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)->x60C`, `temp_r30->parts[var_r27].joint`, `facing_dir`, and floor collision normals, tying the decomp to known struct fields rather than opaque offsets where possible.

The code preserves stack temporary ordering and explicit local `spXX` variables, which is often useful for matching functions that pass addresses of stack floats to variadic or vararg-like effect spawning routines.
decomp_lessons: For large dispatcher functions, an m2c-derived branch skeleton can be a useful intermediate state: even if readability is low, it captures switch/range-test behavior and call-site argument ordering needed for matching.
When implementing a function that calls previously placeholder-only helpers, update the helper prototypes and headers immediately so future files can reuse the same concrete signatures.
Effect-spawning code may require preserving stack locals whose addresses are passed to `efAsync_Spawn`; converting them too aggressively into expressions can harm matching or lifetime behavior.
Floor-aligned visual effects can be recognized through `atan2f(-coll_data.floor.normal.x, coll_data.floor.normal.y)` guarded by `ground_or_air == GA_Ground`.
Randomized effect positions in this area use the pattern `base += 2.0f * range * (HSD_Randf() - 0.5f)` for each axis before passing the resulting `Vec3` stack address to `efAsync_Spawn`.

Special sentinel fighter parts are present: the implementation treats `0x8D` via a cycling `x2220_flag` path and `0x8E` via `temp_r30->ft_data->x8->x10`; these should be revisited when those fields/enums are better named.
The presence of `M2C_ERROR` and `M2C_BITWISE` shows this was not a fully understood/manual cleanup; future work should target these artifacts to improve both match and semantic clarity.
naming_conventions: Fighter common source files retain address-based names such as `ftCo_09F7.c`, `ftCo_0A01.c`, and `ftCo_0B3E.c`.
Function names remain address-derived, e.g. `ftCo_8009F834`, `ftCo_800A0098`, `ftCo_800B463C`, and `ftCo_800B46B8`.

Concrete typedefs/enums are preferred over placeholders when known: `Fighter_GObj`, `ftCo_GObj`, `Fighter_Part`, `Fighter*`, `Vec3*`, `f32`, and `int` replace some `UNK_T`, `UNK_PARAMS`, and raw `int` part usage.
m2c temporary and block names such as `temp_r30`, `var_r27`, `spA8`, and `block_61` were retained in the partially matched function, indicating this PR prioritized matching over final readability.
Header comments continue to record function addresses with comments like `/* 09F834 */` and `/* 0B46B8 */`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used an explicit stack-shape helper struct `_m2c_stack_ftCo_8009F834` documenting inferred float slots from `0x7C` through `0xB0`, likely to reason about stack layout even though the function declares separate locals.
Retained m2c-style `goto` labels and range-test ordering for `arg1` rather than replacing the dispatcher with a semantic switch.
Preserved suspicious artifacts such as `M2C_ERROR`, `M2C_FIELD`, and `M2C_BITWISE` instead of inventing field names or casts without evidence.
Matched vararg-like `efAsync_Spawn` call shapes by passing stack addresses such as `&sp84`, `&sp90`, `&sp7C`, `&sp98`, and fighter field addresses such as `&temp_r30->facing_dir`.

Used constants directly where they appeared in the recovered control flow and calls, including effect IDs/ranges like `0x3E8`, `0x438`, `0x49D`, `0x513`, `0x515`, part sentinels `0x8D`/`0x8E`, and floats `256.0f`, `2.0f`, `0.5f`, `0.017453292f`.

Kept `#pragma force_active on` and existing constants `ftCo_804D87CC`, `ftCo_804D87D0`, and `ftDynamics_803C57B0`, with `OSReport("no effect from animlist %d\n", ...)` used as the default/fallback path.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
There is no explicit reviewer rationale available; conclusions are based on the title, report table, changed files, and diff excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove `M2C_ERROR` or `M2C_BITWISE` from `ftCo_8009F834`.
Find later PRs that name the `0x8D` and `0x8E` `Fighter_Part` sentinel values used by `ftCo_8009F834`.
Search for later cleanup of the `arg1` effect ID dispatcher in `ftCo_09F7.c`.
Search for PRs that decompile or type `ftCo_800B463C` and `ftCo_800B46B8` after this header exposure.
Search for uses of `efAsync_Spawn` with fighter `x60C` to compare argument conventions and effect kind IDs.
